title: Lithium-ion battery with low temperature rapid charge capability
abstract: A lithium-ion battery (10) includes an electrode (14) with microscale channels (24) formed in an electrode material and a nanoscale conformal coating (30) over the electrode material. The channels (24) promote Li-ion transport to the interior of the electrode (14) during charging, and the coating (30) acts as an artificial solid electrolyte interphase (SEI) in place of the SEI that is typically formed during initial charge cycles of a Li-ion battery. The coating (30) can be selected to have a lower impedance than a naturally formed SEI and can be formed in a more controlled manner prior to cell assembly. Cold-charging performance of the resulting battery (10) is enhanced more than would be expected by the individual contributions of the channels (24) and the coating (30).
claims:
Claims 1. A lithium-ion battery (10), comprising: a negative electrode (14) formed from an electrode material; channels (24) formed at least partially through a thickness (TA) of the electrode material; a coating (30) disposed over the electrode material; and a liquid electrolyte infdtrating pores of the electrode, wherein the coating suppresses formation of a solid electrolyte interphase layer from constituents of the liquid electrolyte when the battery is initially charged. 2. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 1, wherein the coating (30) is a solid electrolyte material that reduces interfacial resistance relative to said solid electrolyte interface layer. 3. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 1, wherein each channel (24) is defined by a channel wall (24w), and the coating (30) is disposed along the channel wall. 4. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 3, further comprising a separator (18) confronting a face (26) of the electrode (14), wherein the coating (30) is present along the face of the electrode and extends along each channel wall (24w) from the first face of the electrode. 5. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 1, wherein the coating (30) is a conformal coating on the electrode material such that the electrode material is encapsulated by the coating. 6. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 1, wherein the electrode material comprises graphite. 7. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 6, wherein graphite is the only electrochemically active lithium host material of the electrode material. 8. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 6, wherein the electrode material further comprises silicon or hard carbon. 9. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 1, wherein the coating (30) is glassy lithium borate- 

lithium carbonate. 10. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 1, wherein the battery has an initial charge capacity and a post-cycling charge capacity that is at least 50% of the initial charge capacity after 100 chargedischarge cycles at a cycling temperature less than or equal to 5Â°C and at a charge rate of at least 4C. 11. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 10, wherein the electrode has an areal charge capacity of at least 3 mAh/cm 2 12. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 10, wherein the post-cycling charge capacity is at least 90% of the initial charge capacity. 13. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 10, wherein the cycling temperature is less than or equal to -10Â°C. 14. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 1, wherein each channel (24) has a width in range from 5 pm to 100 pm and each channel is spaced apart from another channel by a distance (X) in a range from 10 pm to 200 pm as measured between centerlines of the channels. 15. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 1, wherein the channels (24) are formed by laser ablation. 16. A lithium-ion battery (10) having an initial charge capacity and a post-cycling charge capacity that is at least 90% of the initial charge capacity after 100 charge-discharge cycles at a cycling temperature less than or equal to 5Â°C and at a charge rate of at least 4C, wherein a negative electrode (14) of the battery has an areal charge capacity of at least 3 mAh/cm 2 . 17. The lithium-ion battery (10) of claim 16, further comprising: a negative electrode (14) formed from a graphite-based electrode material; an array of laser-formed channels (24) extending at least partially through a thickness (TA) of the electrode material, each channel having a width of 100 pm or less; and an artificial sold electrolyte interphase material (30) encapsulating the electrode material and 

having a thickness of 100 nm or less. 18. A method comprising the step of charging a lithium-ion battery (10) at a temperature less than or equal to 5Â°C at a charge rate of at least 4C, wherein the battery is configured to suppress formation of lithium plating on a negative electrode (14) of the battery during the step of charging. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising the steps of: (a) discharging the lithium-ion battery (10) after the step of charging; and (b) repeating the steps of charging and discharging at least 100 times each, wherein the battery is substantially free from lithium plating on the negative electrode (14) after step (b). 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the battery (10) retains at least 90% of an initial charge capacity after step (b). 21. The method of claim 18, further comprising, before the step of charging: (a) constructing the battery (10) from the negative electrode (14), a positive electrode (16), a separator (18), and a liquid electrolyte; and (b) providing the negative electrode (14) with an artificial solid electrolyte interphase coating (30) before step (a), wherein the coating suppresses formation of lithium plating on the negative electrode (14) during the step of charging. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein step (b) includes atomic layer deposition of the coating (30). 23. The method of claim 21, further comprising the step of forming channels (24) at least partially through a thickness (TA) of an electrode material of the negative electrode (14) via laser ablation before step (b).
